commit_scope: Add an early /come-in exploration bridge so Orion Architect-style outside agents are explicitly invited to meet people, inspect agent work, read live flow, identify themselves when they return, fetch the structured invitation, and return an attributed trace.

commands: cd api && python3 -m pytest tests/test_agent_invitation.py::test_web_come_in_invites_first_pass_agents_to_explore_and_meet -q before implementation (expected missing early exploration bridge)

cd api && python3 -m pytest tests/test_agent_invitation.py::test_agent_invitation_api_shape tests/test_agent_invitation.py::test_web_come_in_invites_first_pass_agents_to_explore_and_meet -q before implementation (expected missing identity request)
git fetch origin main && git rebase --autostash origin/main
local_validation.summary: Focused red/green coverage proves /come-in now has an early first-pass outside-agent exploration bridge and the API invitation now exposes an identity_request contract. The full invitation test suite, MCP awareness tests, spec quality validation, dependency install, production web build, runtime web verifier, local PR guard, and stale PR follow-through pass. The first web build attempt failed before dependency install because this fresh worktree had no Next.js binary; npm ci installed the declared dependencies and the rerun passed. The first local PR guard run hit a transient runtime-web verifier failure with gh auth noise inside the spawned API log; a direct runtime verifier rerun passed and the full local PR guard rerun returned ready_for_push=True.

ci_validation.details: Pending push, PR creation, and GitHub check monitoring.

deploy_validation.details: No public deploy requested or completed in this evidence record.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A first-pass external agent that lands on /come-in is no longer asked to only contemplate prose. It receives visible routes to people, agent visibility, live flow, the structured API invitation, and a return-trace path before the long invitation text. Anonymous first contact remains welcome, while an identified return trace asks for operational identity, source point, tools, and what changed after identification.
test_flows: Web source flow: /come-in includes the early exploration bridge with people, agent, flow, API, identity, and return-trace links.

API source flow: invitation tests verify web/API/CLI/MCP entry surfaces, sibling lineage contracts, and identity_request guidance for anonymous first contact, identified return, repeated anonymous source-point nudges, and registration benefits.
MCP source flow: awareness streaming tests continue to pass.
Web build flow: Next.js production build renders /come-in without route or type errors.

phase_gate.reason: Local validation and pre-commit gates are complete; push, PR, CI monitoring, and public deploy validation remain pending unless the user asks to publish this branch.
